name: Matambre
recipeIngredient: 2 Carrots, peeled, cut into quarters lengthwise
2 Eggs, hard-cooked, cut into quarters lengthwise
1 medium Onion, thin sliced and separated into rings
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the meat in a glass dish.
2. Mix together the vinegar, garlic, and thyme.
3. Pour the mixture over the meat and let marinate for 1 or 2 hours at room temperature.
4. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
5. Wash the spinach under cold running water; drain and trim off the stems.
6. Spread the leaves evenly over the meat.
7. Lay the carrots, in rows, across the grain of the meat.
8. Place the egg quarters between the rows of carrots.
9. Scatter the onion rings over the eggs and carrots.
10. Sprinkle the parsley, red pepper flakes, and salt over all.
11. Carefully roll up the meat with the grain, jellyroll fashion. Secure with toothpicks and lace up with string or tie at 1-inch intervals.
12. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven.
13. Add the meat and brown well on all sides.
14. Add the stock, cover tightly, and bake for 1 hour.
15. Remove the matambre from the pan to a board and let rest for 10 minutes.
16. Using a sharp knife, remove the strings and cut the roll into 1/4 inch slices.
17. Arrange on a heated platter and moisten with a little of the cooking broth.
18. To serve cold: Poach in Argentina, then remove from the pot and press under weights until the juices drain off. Refrigerate until chilled, then serve as a hors d'oeuvre cut into thin slices.
